advantages: Provides exact local sensitivity of outputs to infinitesimal input changes—more precise than finite differences at small scales.
Enables efficient optimization via gradient methods instead of blind search over parameter space.
Differentiation rules compose to handle complex expressions built from simple primitives (automatic differentiation).
Connects geometric intuition (tangent slope) with algebraic computation used in every ML framework.
disadvantages: Only describes local behavior—large steps can leave the linear approximation valid region (need learning rate control).
Non-differentiable points (ReLU kinks, max pooling) require subgradient conventions or smooth approximations.
Vanishing or exploding derivatives in deep chains cause training pathologies without architectural mitigations.
Hand-derived derivatives are error-prone for complex models; frameworks help but debugging still requires conceptual understanding.
